title: Designing Robots for Families: In-Situ Prototyping for Contextual Reminders on Family Routines
abstract: 机器人正日益进入家庭日常生活，但其成功融入家庭生活仍面临挑战。本研究以家庭日常作息为切入点，探讨机器人如何在家庭环境中找到可持续的定位。我们与十个家庭共同设计机器人交互行为，并制定支持其选定日常作息的实施方案，同时综合考虑时间安排、参与者、空间位置及环境活动等情境因素。通过设计、原型开发与部署，我们开展了为期四天的移动社交机器人家庭实地研究。家庭成员普遍欢迎机器人的提醒功能，家长尤其肯定其分担提醒任务的作用。与此同时，访谈揭示了时间安排、权限分配与家庭互动关系等方面的矛盾，凸显出机器人融入家庭场景的复杂性远超简单的提醒功能。基于这些发现，我们提出情境化机器人提醒功能的设计方案，并就家庭场景机器人设计的宏观考量展开讨论。
preview_text: Robots are increasingly entering the daily lives of families, yet their successful integration into domestic life remains a challenge. We explore family routines as a critical entry point for understanding how robots might find a sustainable role in everyday family settings. Together with each of the ten families, we co-designed robot interactions and behaviors, and a plan for the robot to support their chosen routines, accounting for contextual factors such as timing, participants, locations, and the activities in the environment. We then designed, prototyped, and deployed a mobile social robot as a four-day, in-home user study. Families welcomed the robot's reminders, with parents especially appreciating the offloading of some reminding tasks. At the same time, interviews revealed tensions around timing, authority, and family dynamics, highlighting the complexity of integrating robots into households beyond the immediate task of reminders. Based on these insights, we offer design implications for robot-facilitated contextual reminders and discuss broader considerations for designing robots for family settings.
one_line_summary: 这篇论文探讨了在家庭环境中设计和部署移动社交机器人以支持日常提醒功能，通过用户研究和原型设计分析机器人融入家庭的挑战，不涉及强化学习、VLA模型或机器人控制等技术主题。
